nativeRange.habitat: Hybrid cultivar with no wild population. Commonly cited as a Heinz Kasselmann selection from the late 1980s involving Echinodorus barthii x E. horemanii 'Red' parentage. Parent genus Echinodorus is generally native to wetlands of the tropical Americas.

decorPreferences: Deep nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or capped sand) — heavy root feeder
Iron-rich root tabs near the root zone are generally considered the key driver of the ruby-red coloration
Open background space with at least 45 cm of vertical clearance
Moderate to bright lighting tends to deepen the red coloration
Moderate flow; avoid placement directly in jet returns
Plant the crown just above the substrate surface — buried crowns are prone to rot

summary: A large hybrid Echinodorus cultivar prized for ruby-red new growth that matures to deep red-green. A classic background sword for medium-to-large planted tanks; iron supplementation is generally regarded as the key driver of the signature color.
careNotes:
Echinodorus 'Red Rubin' is a hybrid Echinodorus cultivar with no wild population — commonly cited as a Heinz Kasselmann selection from the late 1980s, with parentage typically reported as Echinodorus barthii x E. horemanii 'Red', though the exact lineage varies by source. It is sold across the trade as Echinodorus sp. 'Red Rubin' or simply 'Rubin'. Adult height generally runs 25-60 cm submersed, making it one of the larger common swords. 

Care is the standard Echinodorus profile: a heavy root feeder that does best in a deep nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil, capped sand with root tabs, or laterite). The crown should be planted just above the substrate surface — buried crowns tend to rot. Iron-rich root tabs near the root zone are widely regarded as the single biggest driver of the ruby-red coloration; without iron supplementation, new growth has been reported to come in a more olive-green tone. Moderate-to-bright lighting tends to deepen the color further. CO2 is not required but injection has been reported to improve leaf size, density, and color. Sources put temperature around 20-28 C and pH 5.5-8.0, with broad tolerance of gH/kH — generally the same envelope as the rest of the Echinodorus complex. 

A background plant; generally wants at least 45 cm of vertical clearance and a footprint of 30+ cm. Propagation is typically by adventitious plantlets that form on flower stalks or side shoots, and by crown division. Has been reported to be sensitive to copper-based medications. 

Sister entries: fw-plant-011 (Amazon Sword), fw-plant-127 (Ozelot), fw-plant-128 (Rose), fw-plant-142 (Kleiner Bar), fw-plant-150 (Melon), fw-plant-157 (Marble Queen Radican), fw-plant-158 (Red Flame).

primary.name: Modern Aquarium - Red Rubin Echinodorus product/care page
primary.notes: Confirmed rosette growth form, heavy root feeder, moderate light, easy care, runner/plantlet propagation, temp range 68-82 F (20-28 C), iron supplementation for color.

additional.name: Buce Plant - Echinodorus 'Red Rubin' product page
additional.notes: Cross-confirmed family Alismataceae, height 25-61 cm, pH 5.5-8.0, moderate light, heavy root feeder, fast growth, background placement, adventitious-shoot propagation. Also flagged copper sensitivity.
